key_insight: Protecting continual-learning knowledge in LLMs works better in SAE feature space than in weight space because sparse, monosemantic features isolate task-relevant information more precisely.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a more stable and memory-efficient way to prevent catastrophic forgetting in large models, with direct relevance to deploying AI systems that must adapt over time without retraining from scratch.
method: Use pretrained Sparse Autoencoders to convert activations into a monosemantic feature dictionary.
Build a compact task-specific feature mask from current-task data and regularize protected features during later training.
Frame the approach as constrained optimization, with EWC recovered as a special weight-space special case.
evidence_signal: On TRACE and MedCL continual-learning benchmarks, the method outperforms traditional weight-space regularization like EWC and other approaches without task-specific architectural changes.
watch_item: The method depends on a pretrained SAE and a good mask construction step, so performance may be sensitive to feature quality and how well the SAE basis transfers across tasks.
paper_title: From Weights to Features: SAE-Guided Activation Regularization for LLM Continual Learning
